original_text:
### Contrast
- **Test contrast levels** (webaim.com or equivalent) for every text/background pair. [GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.]
- Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy. **Avoid:** Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.
- **Don't rely on color alone** to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

relations.note: The approved and prohibited pairs provide specific contrast guidance under the universal contrast-testing requirement.

quotes: **Test contrast levels** (webaim.com or equivalent) for every text/background pair. [GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.]

rules.intent: Ensure all text remains legible and meets the stated WCAG AA contrast minimums.
rules.rule_text: Test contrast levels using webaim.com or an equivalent tool for every text/background pair. Meet WCAG AA minimums: 4.5:1 for body text and 3:1 for large text at least 24px or 19px bold.

quotes: Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy. **Avoid:** Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Preserve approved readable color pairings and prevent known contrast failures.
rules.rule_text: Use known-safe text/background pairs: Brand Blue (#00529b), Deep Blue (#003d74), Dark Navy (#011e45), or Graphite (#212121) on White (#ffffff), Light Blue (#e6f0f9), or Golden Sand (#fef1c8); and White (#ffffff) on Brand Blue (#00529b), Deep Blue (#003d74), or Dark Navy (#011e45). Do not use Gold (#ffc60a) or Sunshine (#faa31b) text on White (#ffffff), because these fail contrast; these gold tones are reserved for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.
Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Ensure meaning remains understandable for people who cannot distinguish color cues.
rules.rule_text: Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning; every meaningful color cue must be paired with at least one non-color cue, such as an icon, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.
